message:
feat(sync): enforce warmup_timeout on GenericPool warmup phase

Pool warmup now respects PoolConfig::warmup_timeout as a hard deadline
across the entire warmup phase. Each create_resource() call is wrapped
in a timeout using the remaining budget from the warmup deadline. When
a create stalls past the deadline, the behavior depends on the warmup
strategy: FailFast returns PoolError::Timeout immediately, BestEffort
breaks the loop and returns partial progress, RequireMinimum returns
an error only if min_size was not reached.

New TimeoutAfterNSuccessesFactory test helper returns pending futures
after N successful creates, simulating stalled backends. Four new tests
cover all strategy x timeout interactions: fail_fast_returns_timeout,
best_effort_returns_partial_progress, require_minimum_errors_when_min_
not_reached, and require_minimum_keeps_progress_once_min_reached.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
